babyDevelopment: The baby's senses are continuing to develop. Taste buds are forming on the tongue. The reproductive system is maturing—in boys, the testes are beginning to descend; in girls, the ovaries and uterus are in place. Eyes are formed but the lids are still fused.
bodyChanges: Stretch marks might start appearing on your belly, breasts, or thighs. Your heart and lungs are working harder to support the growing baby. You may notice your belly button 'popping' out as the uterus expands.
tips: Moisturize your skin to manage itching and stretch marks
Keep moving to improve circulation and reduce swelling
Ensure you're getting enough magnesium for muscle health

do: Walk periodically if sitting for long periods
dont: Lift heavy objects that strain your core
Worry excessively about stretch marks (mostly genetic)

travelAdvisory.note: Safe to travel; pack compression socks for long trips.

warningSignsToWatch: Sudden, large weight gain in leading to swelling
milestoneDescription: Baby's taste buds and reproductive system are maturing rapidly.
